title: criticality_supplier_heatmap.py output (criticality_supplier_segments.json)
description: Always a flat object — criticality_supplier_heatmap.py requires --component. component_breakdown is one row per supplier (total spend across all criticality tiers); criticality_breakdown is the same spend/share data in long form, one row per (supplier_name, component_criticality) — this is the exact matrix the heatmap image and `segments` below are both built from. segments buckets suppliers by their highest share in any single criticality tier: strategic (>=25%), major (>=10% and <25%), smaller (<10% everywhere) — cross-reference a flagged supplier's qualifying_tiers against criticality_breakdown's share_pct rows for the same supplier to see the full picture.

share_pct.description: This supplier's % share of that criticality tier's total spend.

flagged_supplier.description: A supplier in strategic_suppliers or major_suppliers. qualifying_tiers lists every tier where they crossed that bucket's threshold, richest share first — this is what tells you WHICH tier drove the classification without recomputing the share matrix by hand. CAVEAT: this is a spend-share threshold with no minimum sample-size floor — a supplier can qualify off a single PO if that tier's total spend is small. Cross-check po_count/n_projects from supplier_scorecard before treating the label as meaningful.

component_breakdown.description: One row per supplier: total spend across all criticality tiers in this component.

criticality_breakdown.description: One row per (supplier_name, component_criticality) combination — long form of the heatmap's underlying pivot/share matrices.

share_pct.description: This supplier's % share of this criticality tier's total spend.

smaller_suppliers.description: Flat supplier_name list — no per-tier detail since, by definition, none of these crossed any threshold.
